# Templates

Templates are first-class outputs of this knowledge base. Use them to turn previous work into repeatable project starts, delivery standards, and operational checklists.

Raw copy-ready templates live in the root `templates/` folder. This page explains when to use each one.

## Project Brief

Use before implementation starts. It captures goals, audience, constraints, scope, risks, and success criteria.

Template: `templates/project-brief.md`

## Technical Specification

Use once the project goal is clear and the implementation needs a decision-complete shape.

Template: `templates/technical-specification.md`

## Architecture Decision Record

Use when making a meaningful technical choice that future you will need to understand.

Template: `templates/architecture-decision-record.md`

## Project Setup Checklist

Use at the start of a repo to avoid missing basics like README, env docs, linting, deployment path, and issue tracking.

Template: `templates/project-setup-checklist.md`

## Deployment Checklist

Use before launch or production promotion.

Template: `templates/deployment-checklist.md`

## Client Handover Checklist

Use when closing a client delivery or handing a system to another operator.

Template: `templates/client-handover-checklist.md`

## Postmortem

Use after outages, delivery misses, production issues, or major lessons.

Template: `templates/postmortem.md`

## Tool Evaluation Scorecard

Use before adopting a new tool or replacing an existing one.

Template: `templates/tool-evaluation-scorecard.md`
